// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

{

// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{

  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)

lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)

  decreases |b|
{

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)

      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;

      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;

      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

<DAFNY_CODE>
// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{

  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)

    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;

      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;

    } else {

    }
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)

    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;

      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;

    } else {

      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;

    }
  }
}

lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)

  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;

    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);

          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"

                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)

          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)

// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)

// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)

// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)

// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)

}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]
lemma CountCharStep(s: seq<char>, i: nat, c: char)
  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 1s
lemma CountIntOnesSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 1) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);
    CountIntOnesSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt of a sequence of all 0s
lemma CountIntZerosSeq(n: nat)
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z

// Count occurrences of character c in s
ghost function CountChar(s: seq<char>, c: char): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == c then 1 else 0) + CountChar(s[..|s|-1], c)
}

// Count occurrences of value v in an int sequence
ghost function CountInt(s: seq<int>, v: int): nat
  decreases |s|
{
  if |s| == 0 then 0
  else (if s[|s|-1] == v then 1 else 0) + CountInt(s[..|s|-1], v)
}

// Given letter inventory counts, can we spell `ones` copies of "one" and `zeros` copies of "zero"?
// "one" consumes one each of {o, n, e}; "zero" consumes one each of {z, e, r, o}.
ghost predicate FeasibleCounts(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                         ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  ones <= cn &&
  zeros <= cz &&
  zeros <= cr &&
  ones + zeros <= co &&
  ones + zeros <= ce
}

// Convenience: feasibility directly from the input string
ghost predicate Feasible(s: seq<char>, ones: nat, zeros: nat)
{
  FeasibleCounts(CountChar(s, 'n'), CountChar(s, 'z'), CountChar(s, 'r'),
                 CountChar(s, 'o'), CountChar(s, 'e'), ones, zeros)
}

// Is the binary number [1]^x1 ++ [0]^y1  >=  [1]^x2 ++ [0]^y2 ?
// Among positive binary numbers, longer is larger; at equal length, more leading 1s wins.
ghost predicate BinaryGeq(x1: nat, y1: nat, x2: nat, y2: nat)
{
  if x2 == 0 then true
  else if x1 == 0 then false
  else x1 + y1 > x2 + y2 || (x1 + y1 == x2 + y2 && x1 >= x2)
}

// Helper: CountChar of s[..i+1] in terms of s[..i]

  requires 0 <= i < |s|
  ensures CountChar(s[..i+1], c) == CountChar(s[..i], c) + (if s[i] == c then 1 else 0)
{
  assert s[..i+1][..i] == s[..i];
}

  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 1), 0) == 0
  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 1)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 1);

  }
}

  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 0) == n
  ensures CountInt(seq(n, (i: nat) => 0), 1) == 0

    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce

  decreases n
{
  if n > 0 {
    assert seq(n, (i: nat) => 0)[..n-1] == seq(n-1, (i: nat) => 0);
    CountIntZerosSeq(n-1);
  }
}

// CountInt distributes over concatenation
lemma CountIntConcat(a: seq<int>, b: seq<int>, v: int)
  ensures CountInt(a + b, v) == CountInt(a, v) + CountInt(b, v)
  decreases |b|
{
  if |b| == 0 {
    assert a + b == a;
  } else {
    assert (a + b)[..|a + b| - 1] == a + b[..|b| - 1];
    CountIntConcat(a, b[..|b| - 1], v);
  }
}

// Optimality: the greedy choice (maximize ones first, then zeros) is optimal
lemma OptimalityLemma(cn: nat, cz: nat, cr: nat, co: nat, ce: nat,
                       x: nat, y: nat, xp: nat, yp: nat)
  requires x <= co && x <= cn && x <= ce
  requires x == co || x == cn || x == ce
  requires y <= cz && y <= ce - x && y <= cr && y <= co - x
  requires y == cz || y == ce - x || y == cr || y == co - x
  requires FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, xp, yp)
  ensures BinaryGeq(x, y, xp, yp)
{
  // From feasibility: xp <= cn, xp <= co, xp <= ce (since yp >= 0)
  assert xp <= cn;
  assert xp <= co;
  assert xp <= ce;
  // So xp <= x = min(cn, co, ce)
  assert xp <= x;

  if xp == 0 {
    // BinaryGeq(x, y, 0, yp) is true by definition
  } else {
    // Both x > 0, xp > 0. Need x+y >= xp+yp (then BinaryGeq follows since x >= xp)
    if y == co - x {
      assert x + y == co;
      assert xp + yp <= co;
    } else if y == ce - x {
      assert x + y == ce;
      assert xp + yp <= ce;
    } else if y == cz {
      assert yp <= cz;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    } else {
      assert y == cr;
      assert yp <= cr;
      assert yp <= y;
      assert xp + yp <= x + y;
    }
  }
}

method Cards(s: seq<char>) returns (result: seq<int>)
  requires forall i | 0 <= i < |s| :: s[i] in {'z', 'e', 'r', 'o', 'n'}
  ensures var ones := CountInt(result, 1);
          var zeros := CountInt(result, 0);
          var cn := CountChar(s, 'n');
          var cz := CountChar(s, 'z');
          var cr := CountChar(s, 'r');
          var co := CountChar(s, 'o');
          var ce := CountChar(s, 'e');
          // Result contains only 0s and 1s
          |result| == ones + zeros &&
          // All 1s precede all 0s, forming the maximum binary value for these digit counts
          result == seq(ones, (i: nat) => 1) + seq(zeros, (i: nat) => 0) &&
          // The decomposition is feasible: enough letters to spell ones x "one" + zeros x "zero"
          FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, ones, zeros) &&
          // Optimal: no other feasible decomposition yields a larger binary number
          (forall x: nat, y: nat ::
            FeasibleCounts(cn, cz, cr, co, ce, x, y) ==>
            BinaryGeq(ones, zeros, x, y))
{
  var z: nat := 0;
  var e: nat := 0;
  var r: nat := 0;
  var o: nat := 0;
  var n: nat := 0;

  for i := 0 to |s|
    invariant z
